original_hypothesis.title: RouterV2 Owner Has No Transfer Mechanism — Permanent Single-Key Control Over Critical Configuration
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: RouterV2 uses a simple `owner` variable set in the constructor with no transfer or renounce function. If the owner key is compromised, the attacker can permanently change swapRouter to a malicious contract that steals all tokens approved to the router during concentrated liquidity swaps. If the key is lost, critical parameters can never be updated.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: RouterV2 doesn't inherit OZ Ownable; owner is set once in constructor. setSwapRouter, setAlgebraFactory, setQuoterV2, setAlgebraPoolAPI are all gated by require(msg.sender == owner) with no mechanism to change owner.
original_hypothesis.config_evidence: V1/DeployRouterV2.js shows RouterV2 deployed with constructor args; no post-deploy ownership transfer. Owner is the deployer EOA.

proof_of_signal.pattern: owner = msg.sender in constructor; no transferOwnership or renounceOwnership function anywhere in contract

preconditions: Owner private key is compromised or lost

initial_operational_evidence.suspicious_workflow: No ownership transfer mechanism; permanent single-point-of-failure for router configuration
initial_operational_evidence.admin_dependency: Single EOA controls all router configuration permanently
initial_operational_evidence.exploitation_window: Permanent — if compromised, malicious swapRouter steals approved tokens

operational_questions: Is the router owner a multisig or an EOA?
How many tokens are currently approved to the router for concentrated swaps?
Can the router be replaced without disrupting users?
validation_requirements: Verify no ownership transfer function exists in RouterV2
Check on-chain what address is the current owner

preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_1: Attacker compromises router owner private key
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_3: Next concentrated liquidity swap approves tokens to malicious contract

potential_operational_impact.administrative_compromise: Full control over router's external dependencies
potential_operational_impact.recovery_difficulty: Router must be redeployed; all user approvals must be revoked

investigation_priorities.on_chain_signals: swapRouter address changing to unknown contract
investigation_priorities.operational_anomalies: Unexpected setter function calls from owner

validation_result.impact_summary: Filtered before validation: attack_realism is LOW
validation_result.detailed_analysis: This hypothesis was auto-rejected because its attack_realism grade is LOW. Preconditions require actions outside the attacker's control (off-chain compromise, victim cooperation, or admin collusion). No LLM validation was performed.
